message:
fix(continuous_backpressure): NaN fail-closed across EmaSmoother, sigmoid, and update (ft-yskcu)

Third module in the NaN-poison fail-closed family (after ft-761tz
disk_pressure and ft-atcr0 backpressure). continuous_backpressure had
three separate NaN-propagation paths, each with the same failure
shape: a single NaN observation corrupts internal state forever and
every subsequent output is either NaN or a stale multiply-against-
NaN result.

## EmaSmoother::update — skip NaN samples, preserve prior value

Before: the smoother's update ran alpha.mul_add(raw, (1-alpha)*value)
with no sanitization of raw. A NaN sample set `value` to NaN and
every subsequent update preserved it: `alpha*NaN + (1-alpha)*prev`
is NaN.

After: early-return `self.value` on NaN input (no state change). First
-sample-NaN keeps the smoother uninitialised (value=0.0, initialized
=false) so the next clean sample initialises cleanly from the
first-sample path rather than blending against NaN.

## sigmoid — collapse NaN to the neutral midpoint

The private sigmoid(x) helper previously used .clamp(-500.0, 500.0)
to guard against the f64::exp() overflow path. That's correct for
finite x but does NOT sanitize NaN — f64::clamp(NaN, _, _) returns
NaN, and the subsequent 1.0 / (1.0 + (-NaN).exp()) is NaN too.

Mirror the sibling backpressure_severity sigmoid guard (which this
module was modeled after): return 0.5 (the neutral midpoint) on NaN
input. Inline comment explicitly cites ft-yskcu and notes that
collapsing to 0.5 means "no signal" rather than "saturated either
direction", so a NaN input produces the same throttle action as an
ambiguous-but-finite 0.5 ratio instead of poisoning downstream
multiplications.

## ContinuousBackpressure::update — skip tick on non-finite ratios

Defense in depth even though the smoother is NaN-safe now. Without
the skip, a NaN capture_ratio would:
  - early-return from the smoother (good, value stays clean)
  - but increment update_count (bad — consumers reading the
    snapshot see "fresh update at this tick" when no observation
    actually happened)
  - and still compute_actions() on the unchanged smoother (fine in
    isolation but misleading when the update_count tick says
    otherwise)

Skip the whole update() on any non-finite capture_ratio or
write_ratio so the snapshot stays consistent with "no observation
happened on this tick". Early-return compute_actions() so callers
still get the most recent action set — they just don't see an
incremented update_count claiming a fresh read.

## Five regression tests

All tagged ft_yskcu_ for grep-friendly tracking alongside the
ft_761tz_ suite in disk_pressure.rs:

  - smoother_rejects_nan_sample_and_preserves_prior_value: feed 0.42,
    feed NaN (asserts preserved 0.42), feed 0.50 (asserts blended
    between 0.42 and 0.50).

  - smoother_rejects_nan_first_sample: first sample NaN keeps the
    smoother uninitialised; next clean sample initialises cleanly.

  - (plus 3 more pinning sigmoid's NaN -> 0.5 behavior and
    update()'s skip-on-non-finite behavior — standard pattern
    matching the ft-761tz suite).

Tracks ft-yskcu.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
